commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=76b4b62dcc8054a3f7cbd1106e240c0efe9e6e7a)
commands.review_summary:
The PR modifies cron isolated-agent model preflight to resolve an API key and include a bearer Authorization header on local provider probe requests.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main sends the cron local-provider probe with no headers, and the PR targets that exact call path. I did not establish a live LiteLLM/vLLM reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Contributor action is needed to revise the auth-resolution implementation and provide inspectable real behavior proof before normal maintainer review can continue.

target.title: fix(cron): add Authorization header to provider preflight probe
target.body:
## Summary

The cron job preflight check sends `GET {baseUrl}/models` to verify local provider connectivity before running a job, but was doing so **without an Authorization header**. For providers behind auth middleware (LiteLLM, vLLM, etc.), this generates spurious 401 errors on every cron run.

## Root Cause

`model-preflight.runtime.ts` → `probeLocalProviderEndpoint()` called `fetchWithSsrFGuard` with `init: { method: "GET" }` — no auth header.

## Fix

Replaced the ad-hoc API key resolution with **`resolveApiKeyForProvider()`** — the same function used by normal inference calls. This checks all sources in priority order:
1. `models.providers[provider].apiKey` (config)
2. Environment variables
3. `auth-profiles.json`
4. OAuth tokens
5. Plugin synthetic auth

If no key is found (authless servers like Ollama), probe proceeds without auth — same as before.

## Real behavior proof

### Before
```
GET /models HTTP/1.1  →  401 Unauthorized
POST /chat/completions → 200 OK
```

### After  
```
GET /models HTTP/1.1  →  200 OK
POST /chat/completions → 200 OK
```

Both requests use the same `Authorization: Bearer <key>` header, confirmed by sequential source ports from the same process.

## Verification

Passed 4 independent sub-agent code reviews including import path audit, auth resolution chain verification, local/VPS consistency check, and final strict security review.
target.author: samson1357924
